What's going on everyone? Welcome back to the lead nurtures section for clear definitions. For the purposes of this training, the lead nurture sequence will cover from the point you finished the 10 minute report call to the point that you get on the phone with them for the 60 minute high ticket close, all right? And so that's all the checkpoints that have to happen between then and the sales call. So typically there's a couple of things that need to happen. The first is that they're going to apply to work with you. And so that can either happen in email format or that can happen through an embedded application form, which either can be a woofoo form that you embed into a page or a simple Google form that you can get for free. Either of those would work and you would just send them the link or if you're sending an email, you'd send an actual text email. All right, and I'll get to the scripts for both of those. After that, the last thing that would need to happen is that they have to show up for the appointment that we set for them, right? And so it's just a simple reminder sequence that we have seen that works best for in-person and phone call show-ups. So let's dive in. So there are two options for the application follow-up. So after you've had that initial phone call, the first option is a personalized email. And I use quotes because you're going to use a template more or less and you're going to personalize a couple of things. All right, there are two pros here. Pro number one is that it's very easy tech-wise because you're just emailing someone. And the second one is that they tend to have slightly higher response rates because they're emails and people will see that you took the time to email them and so they will email back. The downside is that sometimes people see long emails and think, oh shoot, this is going to take me a long time. So that's kind of the potential flip negative of the longer email. The fourth point is just that it takes more time for you, if you just send someone an application link, you don't need to personalize it or anything. And so it takes more time for you to make these personalized emails, even if you're just personalizing a template, it still takes time. All right, so those are kind of the pros and cons of the personalized email approach. All right, the second approach is sending them a link to an application and that can be either a Google form or an embedded woofoo form. Woofoo is like very cheap and you can set them up online, very simple. If you're like, I don't know how to do that, Google how to set up a woofoo form and watch a video. It's very straightforward. Google forms are the exact same thing. You can Google, Google forms, and you can create your own form. It's Google, they make things pretty simple. And then you'll have a link that you can send people and then you'll get emailed the responses to those forms to your Gmail account or whatever account you set it up with. So it's very easy and you can even have a redirect after that to lead them to a Facebook group or lead them to a testimonials page or even lead them to schedule a call if you hadn't already done that. So there's some advantages to that. It is a little bit more scalable. If you're doing higher volume, sometimes the application direction is where most people go. The cons are it's that it's a little bit harder tech-wise and I'm saying a little bit because it's 2019. Like none of the things in this training are difficult from a tech standpoint. And if you find them difficult, then you just need to level up your skill set because welcome to the world of business. The con is that links to applications sent in a little bit lower response rate because it's an application that's the same personalized. The pro is that people will perceive it as taking less time and then it does take you less time and it makes it more scalable. Now the objective of this entire process is to clarify their goals, break any beliefs they have and really set you up for the close having kind of gotten some of the obstacles out of the way. And then ultimately the reminder sequence is to get them to show. So this is the email follow-up email. This is the template the template is provided. It's kind of broken into two sections. First is kind of you building rapport showing that you were listening and kind of re-explaining the fact that we're about sustainable transformations and learning habits so you can really solve the problem for good, right? Which is what you should be about anyways. The second is the series of questions that you would want them to answer that kind of is a little bit of an assumed close because when someone actually gets into thinking about the foods they hate and the foods they like and how much they wanna work out and all that kind of stuff, they start kind of automatically assuming they're going to get a plan from you. And so if someone takes the time to fill this out the likelihood that they show up for the appointment is extremely high, all right? And so you'll notice that there are a couple of questions on there near the end that are really important questions which is like that being said, are you willing to, I'm right here in the middle, that being said, are you willing to invest financially and emotionally to achieve your goals? If they say no there, then you're gonna follow up with them and say, hey, got your questions. Can you clarify why it's a no, right? Same thing with the scale of one to 10. If someone says I'm a six, then you'd be like, okay, can you clarify why it's not a 10 because of XYZ that you said to me on the phone, right? Does your spouse support making decisions for your own health or would they need to be on the call? Now, like, don't be a dick. If they say that they would want them on the call because it might affect them, then be totally cool with it. And just say, hey, cool, can they just hop on the same time that we already have scheduled, right? And it's nice to finish the FYI that you accept cards and paper and all that kind of stuff. Obviously put whatever payment methods you do accept on there so that you've already kind of pre-framed the fact that they're going to pay something. So it's not, they're not like blown away by that fact, all right? So that is the follow-up email. It's very straightforward. I put in bold some of the things that you might want to personalize, take it. And the easiest thing to do is just read through this one time when you send it because there are gonna be some things that you might have covered, might not have covered, but this is a very good starting point for you, all right? This took me maybe 10 minutes to write. I write pretty quick, but you can tailor it to them and then it will seem like you wrote an entirely personalized email. And if there's a typo in here, I will try to make sure that it gets fixed before the download that you have, all right? The application, this is a standard woofoo form. Now again, you can do this on a Google form, very, very easy. I'll even show you how easy it is right as soon as we get done with this. But very straightforward in terms of what they need to do. And what we're doing here is we're actually seeding you and we're positioning you. The actual questions in the application really set you up really well. So if we're looking for this, it's like, hey, what program are you applying for? The other options are incredibly important to position you as an expert. So celebrity transformation, NFL combine, consultation, platinum level coaching, all these things are things that are going to position you, right? If other please specify occupation, if they didn't already get this, they would put that in there, which will give you an idea of their income level. What their goal is, obviously we want to capture all of their goals, not just their weight loss goals, cause a lot of times people's goals are far bigger than that. What if they tried in the past, they'll just click all of the things that they have tried. So this is the things that we can use up as ammunition of things they've done in the past that we know they can't turn to as alternatives. So if they're like, well, I'm not really sure, it's like, well, what are you going to go to? Like what's the alternative? Well, let's look at the options, right? You've done this, this and this, and that didn't work, so what are you going to do? And they're like, ah, I was like, right, you need this, right? What stopped you from achieving your goal and or sustaining, which is incredibly important, because honestly the number one thing that we have is that the reason that they're getting on the phone with us is because they either have not achieved their goal or they haven't sustained it, but either way they're not where they want to be. And so you have to get them to admit that and figure out why they gave up, right? What, like, and we seed them a little bit of some of the things they might need because all of these things are going to be provided by the program that we outlined for them, right? What would need to happen for you to look back and think this was the best decision I ever made, I'm so grateful I did this and we're going to use this as like, hey, any year from now, this is what you're going to achieve, right, would that be worth it? Instead it would be the best decision you ever made, right? So all of these things we're using is ammunition to set up the sale, right? Let's get from one to 10, you know, how comfortable are you versus uncomfortable? You want them to be a 10 or a nine here. Is your spouse supportive of your goals? And I even make this a little bit humiliating, million star word, but, you know, do you need permission to do things or can you make your own decisions for your health? So most people are like, I can make my own decisions, but if they do need their spouse then they need to be on the call, right? If select, like, we work off, waiting was primarily, if selected, why would you be a good client? So here it's, again, we're positioning ourselves and then would you be willing to invest financially? And then finally, you know, are you, how soon are you looking to get started? If you're not ready, please wait to schedule. Yes, I'm ready to get started so that they can't say I'm going to need to think about it. And then yes, they understand that you're a real person that they're going to keep their commitments. All right, very, very, very, very straightforward, but these are things that are important to go over and follow up with them. Now, before I get to the reminder sequence, you need to look at these answers before you get on the call with them because you need to make sure that they're qualified to get on the call. And if they are not, you need to qualify the things that you have issues with before you get on the call with them. Most people will want to get on the call after they've filled this application out and so you can make them adjust or change their answers so that when you get on the call, you can have all smooth sailing, right? Or at least stack the chips in your favor so that you can actually help them and really get them out of their own way to help themselves, all right? Now, after we've done this, the three-step reminder sequence is probably the easiest one that I know of. They shouldn't be set more than 48 hours out. So 24 hours beforehand, so if you have a 9 a.m. call like tomorrow, then I would have a 9 a.m. text that I'm gonna send today. Hey, just 24 hour reminder, I'm gonna be talking to you same time tomorrow, okay? And then morning of the actual call. So even if it's in the afternoon, send them a message in the morning, so it reminds them, and then 60 minutes prior, all right? The 24 hour beforehand, I will say that if it isn't in the morning, just a caveat, I found that the night of actually works better. So I will probably edit this in the actual training, but when I think 24 hours, most people have things in the afternoon, so I like to do it the night before so that's when people are thinking about what they have to do the next day. Morning of is because now they've started their day and we need to get on their minds again, and the 60 minutes prior is like, hey, heads up, stop what you're doing because in an hour we're gonna be on the phone, okay? So that's kind of the three points that we've seen are the biggest pivots for making sure that people show up to the appointments that they sat with you. So if you just do those three manually, you'll be in a good spot.
